title: centaurea scabiosa subsp. fritschii
intro: Centaurea scabiosa subsp. fritschii, also known as Cornflower or Fritsch's knapweed, is a subspecies of flowering plant that belongs to the Asteraceae family. This plant is valued for its medicinal properties, particularly its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which can be used to treat various health issues. The health benefits of Centaurea scabiosa subsp. fritschii include its ability to alleviate digestive issues, reduce fever, and soothe respiratory problems. Traditionally, herbal preparations of this plant are made into teas, infusions, and tinctures to harness its medicinal properties, although further research is needed to confirm its efficacy and safety.
